conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules to ensure I can give you accurate guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have a child or children you're responsible for? If yes, could you tell me how many children you have and their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - Alex, who is 18 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To determine Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to understand their education or training status.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education - he's studying for his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. And is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000? (This won't affect your eligibility, but it may mean you need to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not get into the income details right now - I'm mainly just trying to find out if I'm eligible to claim in the first place.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's absolutely fine - I have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided, let me evaluate your situation:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: Even though Alex is 18 and over the usual age limit of 16, you still qualify for Child Benefit because Alex is under 20 years old and is in approved education (studying for A-levels). Since you live with Alex in the UK and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, you meet all the eligibility requirements.

**Important note:** Child Benefit will continue as long as Alex remains in approved education or training and is under 20 years old. If Alex leaves education or turns 20, your entitlement would stop.

If you'd like to proceed with a claim, you can apply through the official government website or contact the Child Benefit Office directly.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and still in approved education (studying for A-levels). According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. However, you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex meets this criteria as he is 18 (under 20) and is in approved education. You live with Alex in the UK, making you responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old child, Alex, who is currently studying for A-levels (approved education). Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and still in approved education (studying for A-levels). According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. However, you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex meets this criteria as he is 18 (under 20) and is in approved education. You live with Alex in the UK, making you responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old child, Alex, who is currently studying for A-levels (approved education). Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
